title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
The Earl of Carringham, feeling anxious and complaining about Romax, joins Bunder and Bondell for breakfast in the dining room. The white-haired butler, Tredwell, serves various dishes, but the Earl expresses dissatisfaction with the food and angrily rejects a dish of poached eggs. During the meal, the Earl expresses frustration regarding his social status and his involvement in a current "mess," mentioning that Dr. Abner Willis has advised him not to worry. The group discusses the recent death of an individual, with the Earl suspecting Esarhaddon, a representative of the Anglo-American Bank Group, might be responsible.

Mr. Kodges arrives at the villa in a speeding car and enters the dining room in a state of distress, calling the situation a national disaster and claiming that backup plans have been sabotaged. He insists that the matter be kept secret. George (Romax) suggests they need Inspector Batter from Scotland Yard rather than the local police. George writes a telegram to Lord Lolopryte Jeechill and instructs the Earl of Carterham to give him full control of the situation. The Earl of Carterham agrees, entrusting the chimney villa to George before slipping away.

Later, the Earl of Carringham attempts to avoid George by wandering his estate and sneaking into a secret room through a side door. However, Tredwell intercepts him and informs him that Romax is eager to see him in the study. The Earl reluctantly agrees to meet him.
